distribution_driven: Function-driven in mechanism but data-driven in placement: the annotation engine itself is fixed (it just draws callouts at supplied x/y/dx/dy), but which points get a callout is chosen from the actual distribution (the analyst picks the 10 outliers). It does not transform the scale, so the underlying distribution is preserved unaltered.
d3_api: Plugin (d3-annotation / npm package d3-svg-annotation). Core pattern: const makeAnnotations = d3.annotation().type(d3.annotationCalloutCircle).accessors({ x: d => xScale(d.amount), y: d => yScale(d.y) }).annotations(annotationsArray); then svg.append('g').attr('class','annotation-group').call(makeAnnotations). Annotation objects: { note: { title, label, wrap, align, orientation }, x, y, dx, dy, subject: { radius, radiusPadding } for annotationCalloutCircle, or subject: { width, height } for annotationCalloutRect }. annotationCalloutCircle draws a circle around a subject point; annotationCalloutRect draws a bounding rectangle. Other built-ins: annotationLabel, annotationCallout, annotationCalloutElbow, annotationCalloutCurve, annotationXYThreshold, annotationBadge. Annotations are draggable in editMode via .editMode(true). d3.annotationCustomType() extends the set.
library_dependency: Plugin. Requires d3-annotation (published as d3-svg-annotation on npm, ~13KB min). Built for d3-v4 but works with d3-v7 since it only consumes d3-selection/d3-drag/d3-dispatch idioms. NOT core d3.
zero_negative_handling: Does not fail on ≤0. The plugin performs no scale transform; it draws callouts at pixel coordinates produced by whatever scale you pass (linear, symlog, etc.). Zero and negative loan values render fine as long as the underlying scale maps them. Honesty/legibility of the cramped region is governed by that scale, not by the annotation layer.
authority_verdict: Tufte-aligned: consistent with Tufte's principle of labeling data in place and maximizing data-ink rather than rescaling or hiding outliers ("The Visual Display of Quantitative Information", 1983, and "Visual Explanations", 1997 — annotation/layering and small directly-labeled notes). Cleveland ("The Elements of Graphing Data", 1985/1994) does not address this plugin but his table-look-up principle is served by attaching the exact value ("$1.2M") to the mark. No authority warns against direct callouts; the caution is that callouts alone do not fix the compressed main band — they complement, not replace, a scale/break strategy. [uncertain]
tradeoffs: (1) Honesty: highest of all techniques — nothing is rescaled, capped, or broken, so magnitude perception is undistorted; the outlier truly sits 200x up the axis and the callout merely names it. (2) Readability: does NOT solve the core compression problem on its own — if 84% of loans sit in a $0–$10K band squashed against the axis, callouts on the 10 outliers leave that band unreadable. Best paired with symlog/log/broken-axis. (3) Comprehension: very high — callout-with-label is universally understood by untrained readers; editorial title + value requires no legend.
observable_urls: https://github.com/susielu/d3-annotation (762 stars, 74 forks, last release v2.3.2 Mar 2019, low maintenance); https://d3-annotation.susielu.com/ (official docs/examples, 762 stars upstream); https://observablehq.com/@hydrosquall/d3-annotation-with-d3-line-chart (integration example with a line chart) [uncertain on notebook fork count]
community_reception: 762 GitHub stars, 74 forks on susielu/d3-annotation as of June 2026; widely cited in D3 tutorials and the de-facto annotation library for the D3 ecosystem despite being unmaintained since 2019 (built for v4). Appears in numerous Observable notebooks and the susielu.com gallery. [uncertain on exact current star count drift]
accessibility_support: The plugin emits SVG <g>/<text>/<path> with class hooks but no built-in ARIA. Recommended pattern: set role="img" + aria-label on the chart container; give each callout group an aria-label or per-mark <title>/aria-labelledby naming the outlier and value ("Loan #14, $1.2M"); and supply a visually-hidden data table fallback (the community-recommended approach for complex D3, per fossheim.io). Callout text is real SVG <text>, so screen readers can reach it if the group is exposed, but keyboard focusability must be added manually (tabindex). [uncertain]
best_for: Best as a complement, not a standalone fix. For the 200-loan dataset (84% <$10K, 10 outliers to $1.2M), use annotationCalloutCircle on each of the 10 outliers — atop a symlog or broken-axis base — to attach the literal value ("≥ $120K", "$1.2M") and an editorial note. It is the honest, editorial layer that names extremes; it does NOT decompress the dense sub-$10K band, so it should always sit on a scale/break technique that does.
